proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with feature_013 = 1 have a lower probability of objective_response than those with feature_013 = 0.
Patients with feature_067 = 1 have a lower probability of objective_response than those with feature_067 = 0.
Patients with feature_006 = 1 have a higher probability of objective_response than those with feature_006 = 0.
Patients with feature_007 = 1 have a higher probability of objective_response than those with feature_007 = 0.
Patients with feature_039 = 1 have a higher probability of objective_response than those with feature_039 = 0.

analyses.result_summary: feature_013=1 vs 0: response rate 15.4% vs 19.6% (n=32365 vs 17635). Logistic OR=0.744, p=1.3e-33.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher feature_011 is associated with lower probability of objective_response (continuous, monotonic).
Higher feature_099 is associated with higher probability of objective_response.
Higher feature_063 is associated with lower probability of objective_response.
Higher feature_092 is associated with higher probability of objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: feature_011 mean 3.38 in responders vs 3.93 in non-responders. OR per SD = 0.86, p=1.8e-32.
feature_099 slightly higher in responders (3.82 vs 3.80). OR per SD = 1.047, p=1.4e-4.
feature_063 mean 5.78 in responders vs 6.15 in non-responders. OR per SD = 0.957, p=6.9e-4.
feature_092 marginally higher in responders. OR per SD = 1.036, p=3.2e-3.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_051 (an ordinal 0/1/2 variable, plausibly performance status) is monotonically negatively associated with objective_response.
Histology (feature_043 squamous vs adenocarcinoma) is associated with objective_response.
Smoking status (feature_057 current vs former vs never) is associated with objective_response.
Race (feature_123) is associated with objective_response.
Insurance type (feature_005) is associated with objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: Response rates by feature_051: level 0=21.1% (n=17557), level 1=15.7% (n=24962), level 2=11.1% (n=7481). Per-level OR=0.687, p=1.7e-93. Strongest single predictor identified.
Squamous 17.4% vs adenocarcinoma 16.7%. Chi-square p=0.078; trivial difference, not significant.
Current 17.2%, former 16.9%, never 16.4%. Chi-square p=0.27; not significant.
Race-stratified rates: white 16.6%, hispanic 17.2%, black 17.8%, asian 17.7%, other 18.5%. Chi-square p=0.045 (borderline). White slightly lower than non-white (~1.2 pp).
Medicaid 16.1%, Medicare 16.8%, private 17.2%, uninsured 17.0%. Chi-square p=0.23; not significant overall.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_013, feature_067, feature_006, feature_007, feature_039, feature_011, feature_099, feature_063, feature_092, and feature_051 each retain independent association with objective_response after mutual adjustment.
After adjusting for clinical features, race (feature_123) no longer differs significantly across objective_response.
After adjusting for clinical features, private insurance (feature_005=private) is associated with higher objective_response than medicaid.

analyses.result_summary: All ten primary predictors remain independently significant in the 22-df adjusted model. Adjusted ORs: feature_013 0.74 (p=2e-34), feature_067 0.77 (p<1e-18), feature_006 1.14 (p<1e-5), feature_007 1.08 (p=8e-4), feature_039 1.08 (p=1e-3), feature_051 level 1 OR=0.69, level 2 OR=0.46 (both p<1e-100), feature_011 OR per unit 0.961 (p<1e-32), feature_099 OR=1.10 (p=1e-4), feature_063 OR per unit 0.995 (p=5e-4), feature_092 OR=1.19 (p=2e-3). Pseudo-R2 ~0.020.
Adjusted race contrasts vs Asian (reference): black p=0.94, hispanic p=0.49, other p=0.62, white p=0.13. No significant race disparity remains after clinical adjustment.
Adjusted vs Medicaid: private OR=1.093 (p=0.019), Medicare OR=1.065 (p=0.10), uninsured OR=1.086 (p=0.23). Modest but statistically significant private-vs-medicaid advantage.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The effect of feature_013 on objective_response differs between adenocarcinoma and squamous histology (feature_043).
The effect of feature_067 on objective_response differs by histology (feature_043).
The effect of feature_006 on objective_response differs by histology (feature_043).
The effect of feature_007 on objective_response differs by histology (feature_043).
The effect of feature_039 on objective_response differs by histology (feature_043).

analyses.result_summary: Adeno OR=0.735 (p=1e-26), squamous OR=0.769 (p=1e-8). Interaction beta=0.046, p=0.40. No heterogeneity.
Adeno OR=0.783, squamous OR=0.762. Interaction p=0.66. No heterogeneity.
Adeno OR=1.142 (p=2e-5), squamous OR=1.111 (p=0.025). Interaction p=0.62.
Adeno OR=1.077 (p=0.009), squamous OR=1.110 (p=0.020). Interaction p=0.57.
Adeno OR=1.063, squamous OR=1.096. Interaction p=0.58.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The effect of feature_013 on objective_response differs by smoking status (feature_057).
The effect of feature_006 on objective_response is greater in current/former smokers than in never-smokers (smoking is a known immunotherapy benefit modifier).
The effect of feature_007 on objective_response differs by smoking status, specifically attenuated in never-smokers.

analyses.result_summary: Smoking-stratified ORs: current 0.732, former 0.741, never 0.785. Interaction min p=0.37. No meaningful heterogeneity.
Current smokers OR=1.149 (p=1e-3), former 1.114 (p=3e-3), never 1.213 (p=0.08; n=7459, weaker power). Interaction p=0.59. Effect numerically similar across groups.
Current OR=1.10, former 1.10, never 1.006 (p=0.93). Interaction min p=0.23. Suggestive nominal attenuation in never-smokers but not statistically significant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_006 and feature_039 act synergistically: the effect of feature_006 on objective_response is amplified when feature_039 = 1.
feature_007 and feature_039 act synergistically on objective_response.
feature_006 and feature_007 act synergistically on objective_response.

There is a three-way synergistic interaction among feature_006, feature_007, and feature_039: only patients with all three positive show a meaningfully elevated objective_response rate; patients positive for any one or two show response near baseline.

analyses.result_summary: Three-way interaction beta=+0.392, p=1.8e-4. Cell-wise response: all-zero=16.1% (n=9882); single-positive cells 16.2-16.9%; double-positive cells 16.5-16.9%; all-three-positive=23.0% (n=3216). Pattern is genuinely synergistic, not additive — having all three jumps response by ~7 percentage points.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Composite indicator triplet_pos = (feature_006=1 AND feature_007=1 AND feature_039=1) is associated with higher objective_response.
Number of positive markers among {feature_006, feature_007, feature_039} (count 0/1/2/3) is monotonically associated with objective_response.
The effect of triplet_pos on objective_response remains significant after adjusting for prognostic features feature_013, feature_067, feature_011, and feature_051.

analyses.result_summary: Triplet_pos=1 (n=3216) response 23.0% vs triplet_pos=0 (n=46784) response 16.5%. OR=1.51, p=3.0e-21.

By count: 0=16.1% (n=9882), 1=16.4% (n=21815), 2=16.8% (n=15087), 3=23.0% (n=3216). Linear OR per +1 marker = 1.10 (p=1e-10), but the relationship is clearly non-linear — almost all the lift is in the 3-positive cell, supporting the threshold/synergy interpretation.

analyses.code: smf.logit('objective_response ~ triplet_pos + feature_013 + feature_067 + feature_011 + C(feature_051)', df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted OR for triplet_pos = 1.53, p=1.2e-21 (beta=+0.422). Effect not explained by performance status, NLR-like marker, or other prognostics.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The benefit of triplet_pos (higher objective_response) is greater in patients with worse performance status (feature_051=2) than in better-PS patients (feature_051=0).
The benefit of triplet_pos differs by histology (feature_043).
The benefit of triplet_pos differs by smoking status (feature_057), particularly attenuated in never-smokers.

analyses.result_summary: Stratified ORs: feature_051=0: OR=1.42 (p=4e-7); =1: OR=1.50 (p=2e-10); =2: OR=1.95 (p=9e-8). Interaction triplet_pos:feature_051[T.2] beta=+0.32, p=0.026 — triplet effect is significantly amplified at the worst PS level.
Adeno: triplet OR=1.49 (p=2e-13). Squamous: OR=1.55 (p=4e-9). Effect magnitude similar; no histology heterogeneity (interaction p~0.7, not formally tested but visually concordant).
Current smokers: OR=1.56 (p=6e-12); former: 1.49 (p=3e-10); never: 1.24 (p=0.33, n=139 triplet-positive). Numerically attenuated in never-smokers but underpowered; no formal interaction sig.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The triplet_pos response benefit differs across racial groups (feature_123).
The triplet_pos response benefit differs across insurance categories (feature_005).

analyses.result_summary: Triplet OR within race: white 1.52 (p=1e-14), black 1.64, hispanic 1.38, asian 1.46, other 1.69. None of the four interaction terms reaches p<0.5. No racial disparity in triplet benefit.
Triplet OR within insurance: medicaid 1.57, medicare 1.43, private 1.57, uninsured 1.75. All interaction p > 0.4. No insurance-related disparity in triplet benefit.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_051 (PS) effect on objective_response is greater in squamous histology than adenocarcinoma.
feature_051 (PS) effect on objective_response differs by race (feature_123).

analyses.result_summary: Interaction terms: feature_051[T.1]:squamous beta=-0.087, p=0.12; feature_051[T.2]:squamous beta=-0.06, p=0.52. Trend toward steeper PS gradient in squamous, but not statistically significant.
All eight interaction terms p>0.4. PS gradient is consistent across all racial groups.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_011 has a non-linear relationship with objective_response (e.g., quadratic or threshold).
Tertile 3 (high) of feature_011 has lower objective_response than tertile 1 (low).

analyses.result_summary: Linear term beta=-0.044, p=1e-7; quadratic term beta=+0.0005, p=0.46. No evidence of non-linearity — log-odds is monotone linear in feature_011.
Tertile RRs: low 19.1%, mid 17.0%, high 14.5%. Mid vs low OR=0.86 (p=3e-7); high vs low OR=0.72 (p<1e-29). Strong monotone gradient confirms refined direction.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Age (feature_078, ~normally distributed around 65 years) is associated with objective_response.
The benefit of triplet_pos differs by age (feature_078) quartile.

analyses.result_summary: Age quartile RRs: q1 16.5%, q2 17.5%, q3 16.6%, q4 16.9%. q2 vs q1 nominally elevated (p=0.04); other contrasts NS. No clinically meaningful age effect on response.
Triplet:age interaction terms: all four interaction p > 0.5. Triplet effect is age-invariant (~OR 1.5 across all quartiles).

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_013 and feature_067 (both negative prognostic) interact synergistically on objective_response — patients with both have a worse-than-additive deficit.

analyses.result_summary: Cell RRs: (013,067)=(0,0)=20.7% (n=13223); (0,1)=16.5%; (1,0)=16.1%; (1,1)=13.2% (n=8172). Interaction beta=+0.039, p=0.51. Effects are essentially additive on the log-odds scale; no synergy.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The benefit of triplet_pos is reduced in patients with feature_013=1 (negative prognostic marker).
The benefit of triplet_pos is reduced in patients with feature_067=1.
The benefit of triplet_pos differs across tertiles of feature_011.

analyses.result_summary: Triplet effect: in feature_013=0 OR=1.36, in feature_013=1 OR=1.61. Interaction beta=+0.167, p=0.064 — borderline; if anything triplet benefit is slightly larger in feature_013=1 (opposite of hypothesized direction).
Triplet effect: in feature_067=0 OR=1.50, in feature_067=1 OR=1.58. Interaction p=0.61. No meaningful modification.
Triplet OR: low feature_011 1.53, mid 1.44, high 1.58. Interaction terms p=0.58 and p=0.77. No heterogeneity by feature_011 tertile.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among the strong continuous predictors, feature_063 and feature_092 interact in their effect on objective_response.
feature_011 effect modifies effects of the binary triplet markers feature_006, feature_013, feature_067 (continuous-by-binary interactions).

analyses.result_summary: Interaction beta=+0.014, p=0.040. Borderline-significant continuous-continuous interaction; magnitude small.

analyses.code: Three logit models with feature_011 * each binary
analyses.result_summary: feature_011 x feature_013 int p=0.54; feature_011 x feature_067 int p=0.89; feature_011 x triplet_pos int p=0.72. No effect modification by feature_011 across these binaries.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Other binary features (feature_076, feature_112, feature_021) have weak but real associations with objective_response that survive multivariable adjustment.

analyses.result_summary: feature_076 (n+=4066) RR 18.2% vs 16.8% (OR=1.11, p=0.018); feature_112 (n+=3879) RR 15.6% vs 17.0% (OR=0.91, p=0.031); feature_021 (n+=9048) OR=0.94 (p=0.049). All borderline; would not survive Bonferroni across 77 binaries (threshold p<6.5e-4). Treat as noise.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Other ordinal/integer features (feature_026, feature_033, feature_064, feature_018, feature_045, feature_042) — likely staging/severity scores — have monotone associations with objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: Per-level ORs and p-values: feature_026 0.99 (p=0.24); feature_033 0.98 (p=0.04); feature_064 1.00 (p=0.78); feature_018 0.99 (p=0.49); feature_045 1.01 (p=0.26); feature_042 0.99 (p=0.35). Only feature_033 reaches p<0.05 nominally; would not survive multiple-comparison correction. None show the steep gradient seen in feature_051. Best interpretation: feature_051 alone captures performance status; the others are not clinically informative for response.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Final adjusted multivariable model: triplet_pos, feature_013, feature_067, feature_011, feature_099, feature_063, feature_092, feature_051, and insurance type each retain independent effects on objective_response. Race, smoking, histology, and age do not.

analyses.code: smf.logit('objective_response ~ triplet_pos + feature_013 + feature_067 + feature_011 + feature_099 + feature_063 + feature_092 + C(feature_051) + C(feature_043) + C(feature_057) + C(feature_123) + C(feature_005) + feature_078', df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted ORs (significant): triplet_pos 1.53 (p=1.7e-21); feature_051[T.1] 0.69, feature_051[T.2] 0.46 (both p<1e-30); feature_013 0.74 (p=1e-34); feature_067 0.77 (p=3e-19); feature_011 OR per unit 0.961 (p<1e-32); feature_099 OR 1.10 (p=1e-4); feature_063 OR 0.995 (p=4e-4); feature_092 OR 1.19 (p=2e-3); private vs medicaid OR 1.09 (p=0.019). Non-significant: histology (p=0.16), smoking (both p>0.7), race (all p>0.13), age (p=0.80), medicare (p=0.10), uninsured (p=0.24).

proposed_hypotheses.text: When the three-way interaction term is fitted explicitly alongside main effects, the three pairwise interactions among {feature_006, feature_007, feature_039} drop out and only the three-way term remains significant — confirming a true higher-order pattern rather than confounded pairwise effects.

analyses.code: smf.logit('objective_response ~ feature_006 * feature_007 * feature_039 + ... covariates', df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: In the saturated model: each main effect (006/007/039) p>0.17; each pairwise interaction p>0.38; THREE-way term beta=+0.419, p=7.5e-5. After controlling for clinical covariates the three-way term remains highly significant. This is mechanistically consistent with a regimen or composite biomarker that requires all three components to be present (e.g., a 3-marker treatment indicator or a triple-positive signature).

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_086 (a binary with prevalence 55%, the closest of any binary to 50% — plausibly sex) is associated with objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: feature_086 = 1 (n=27510) RR 16.8% vs 0 (n=22490) RR 17.0%. OR=0.99, p=0.71. No association. Cross-tabs against histology and smoking show feature_086 is essentially independent of those (proportions ~55:45 within every level), so feature_086 likely is not a strong demographic axis for this outcome — or, if it is sex, then sex does not differentially predict response in this cohort.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_092 (continuous, OR=1.19 unit, range 0-0.8) is a fractional/proportion-like marker with a positive monotone effect; per-tertile analysis confirms a top-tertile vs bottom-tertile elevated response.

feature_099 (continuous, mean ~3.8, narrow SD 0.5; possibly a serum value like albumin) has an independent positive relationship with objective_response after adjusting for feature_011 and other prognostic variables.

analyses.result_summary: Tertile RRs: low 16.4%, mid 16.9%, high 17.6%. Top vs bottom OR=1.09 (p=4e-3). Modest but real positive monotone gradient.

Adjusted OR per unit feature_099 = 1.097 (p=1e-4) in the full model — independent of feature_011 (NLR-like), feature_063, feature_092, and PS (feature_051). Consistent with feature_099 being a distinct biomarker (perhaps albumin: typical NSCLC range, narrow SD).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Accounting for the prognostic profile (feature_013, feature_067, feature_011, feature_051), no demographic group (race, insurance) and no clinical-stage variable (histology, smoking, age) shows residual disparity in objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: After adjustment for the strong clinical/biological predictors: race contrasts all p>0.13 (white vs asian beta=-0.077; not significant); smoking former/never each p>0.7; histology squamous p=0.16; age p=0.80. The only residual demographic effect is private-vs-medicaid OR=1.09 (p=0.019), modest in magnitude. Conclude: this cohort shows minimal demographic disparity in response after clinical adjustment.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Synthesis hypothesis: objective_response in this NSCLC cohort is driven by (a) a small set of independent biological/lab markers (feature_013, feature_067 negative; feature_006/007/039 positive when triple-positive; feature_011, feature_063 negative continuous; feature_099, feature_092 positive continuous) and (b) performance status (feature_051), with virtually no contribution from demographics. The triple-marker synergy among feature_006/007/039 is the most clinically interesting finding — it identifies a ~6.4% subset (n=3216) with substantially higher response (23.0%) than the rest of the cohort (16.5%).

analyses.code: Composite review of all prior analyses
analyses.result_summary: Across 25 iterations the model with the largest effect sizes is: triplet_pos OR=1.53 in adjusted model; feature_051 level 2 OR=0.46 (largest single OR in cohort); feature_013 OR=0.74; feature_067 OR=0.77. Triplet effect is amplified at worst PS (interaction p=0.026). Demographics, histology, smoking, and age contribute no meaningful adjusted effect on response. The non-linear count→response relationship (RRs 16.1% / 16.4% / 16.8% / 23.0% for 0/1/2/3 markers) supports a true synergistic/threshold biology rather than additive marker burden.
